zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford於1410年出生在蘇格蘭Forfarshire福爾法爾時，她的父親Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford25歲，母親Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness16歲。她於大約1436年與Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1462年2月8日在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯享年52歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford于1410年出生在苏格兰Forfarshire福爾法爾时，她的父亲Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford25岁，母亲Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness16岁。她于大约1436年与Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1462年2月8日在英国苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯享年52岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: Margaretha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford was born in 1410, in Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, her father, Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford, was 25 and her mother, Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness, was 16. She married Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater about 1436, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 8 February 1462, in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford est née en 1410, à Forfar, Forfarshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater environ 1436, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 février 1462, à Douglas, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford 1410 in Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford 25 und ihre Mutter Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness 16. Sie hat um 1436 in Scotland Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. Februar 1462 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford è nata il nel 1410 nel Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, sua padre, Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness, aveva 16 anni. Lei sposato Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater circa 1436, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 8 febbraio 1462, nel Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskfordが1410年にスコットランドForfarshireフォーファルで生まれた時，父Sir John Sinclair Of Deskfordは25歳，母Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithnessは16歳であった。約1436年にスコットランドでWalter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlaterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1462年2月8日にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラスで52歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford(이)가 1410년 스컷랜드 Forfarshire 포파르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1436년 스컷랜드에서 Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1462년 2월 8일 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford nasceu em 1410, em Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness, tinha 16 anos. Ela casou-se com Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater aproximadamente 1436, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de fevereiro de 1462, em Douglas, Lanarkshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 52 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford родилась в 1410 г. в местности форфар, Forfarshire, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater около 1436 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 8 февраля 1462 г. в местности доуглас, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaretha Sinclair of Deskford nació en 1410, en Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir John Sinclair Of Deskford, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mary Elizabeth Douglas Countess of Buchan, Orkney and Caithness, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Walter Ogilvy 1st of Deskford and Findlater aproximadamente 1436, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 8 de febrero de 1462, en Douglas, Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
